evals.name: react18-integration
evals.prompt: I'm adding SparkScan to my React 18.3 + Vite app. Here's my component (empty-react-app.tsx) and my package.json. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes.

assertions.text: Determines the React major version from package.json (recognizes React 18)
Binds dataCaptureContext and sparkScan via the element ref (assigns el.sparkScan / el.dataCaptureContext as properties), NOT as declarative JSX attributes
Does NOT pass dataCaptureContext/sparkScan as plain JSX props like <spark-scan-view dataCaptureContext={...} sparkScan={...}> (which would silently fail on React 18)
Both dataCaptureContext and sparkScan are bound via the ref so the view prepares (calling prepareScanning() explicitly is acceptable; a specific assignment order is not required)
DataCaptureContext is configured once for the app (module-scope-once or a React context provider), not re-created per component mount
DataCaptureContext is NOT disposed on component unmount
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is present with libraryLocation and moduleLoaders including barcodeCaptureLoader()
SparkScanSettings is created and enableSymbologies enables Symbology.EAN13UPCA and Symbology.Code128 (and no others)
SparkScan.forSettings( is present
addListener with a didScan callback is present and removeListener is called in cleanup
SparkScanView.register() is called, guarded against re-registration on HMR (a customElements.get("spark-scan-view") check or a try/catch is acceptable)

evals.name: react19-integration
evals.prompt: Add SparkScan to my React 19 app (Vite). package.json attached. I want to scan QR codes. Here's my component: empty-react-app.tsx.

assertions.text: Determines the React major version from package.json (recognizes React 19)
Uses a valid binding for React 19 — either declarative property props (dataCaptureContext={...} sparkScan={...}) or ref-binding; both acceptable on 19
DataCaptureContext is configured once for the app (module-scope-once or a React context provider)
DataCaptureContext is NOT disposed on component unmount
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is present with moduleLoaders including barcodeCaptureLoader()
Symbology.QR is enabled (and no unrelated symbologies)
SparkScan.forSettings( is present and SparkScanView.register() is called
addListener/removeListener lifecycle is handled

evals.name: works-then-dies-singleton
evals.prompt: My React SparkScan app works for a second, then dies — in dev the console shows 'forLicenseKey ... already initializing'. Here's the component (broken-react-app.tsx) and my package.json. Please fix it.

assertions.text: Identifies the root cause: DataCaptureContext is a process-wide singleton being disposed on component unmount
Mentions React StrictMode's dev mount/unmount/remount as a contributing factor
Removes the context.dispose() call from the unmount cleanup
Moves one-time configuration into a React context provider (or a module-scope-once initializer) so forLicenseKey runs once for the app
Consumes the context via DataCaptureContext.sharedInstance rather than storing/disposing the instance
Because package.json is React 18, converts the declarative props to ref-binding (assigns element properties via a ref)
Preserves the enabled symbologies and the scan listener

evals.name: custom-trigger-prepare-lifecycle
evals.prompt: I built a custom trigger button that drives scanning myself (I hid the default trigger). After the user stops scanning and taps to start again, I get the error 'prepare should be called before calling switchToActiveState'. How do I fix my start/stop logic? Here's my file: integrated-app.ts.

assertions.text: Explains that stopScanning() un-prepares the view, so a later startScanning() throws unless the view is prepared again
Calls prepareScanning() before startScanning() when restarting after a stop
Correctly states that pauseScanning() keeps the view prepared, so no re-prepare is needed after a pause (does not add a redundant prepareScanning() after pauseScanning)
Hides the default trigger with triggerButtonVisible = false (or confirms it is hidden) for the custom-trigger setup
Preserves existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, listener, mount/unmount)

evals.name: camera-not-opening-on-device
evals.prompt: SparkScan works on localhost, but the camera never opens when I load the app on my phone at http://192.168.1.42:5173. It's a Vite app. What's wrong and how do I fix it? package.json attached.

assertions.text: Identifies the secure-context requirement: getUserMedia only runs in a secure context, and a plain http:// LAN IP is not secure (localhost is exempt)
Recommends serving the dev app over HTTPS to fix it
Because the project uses Vite, recommends the @vitejs/plugin-basic-ssl plugin (or otherwise enabling https on the Vite dev server)
Mentions exposing the dev server on the LAN (e.g. server.host) or a tunneling option such as portless.sh
Does NOT misdiagnose it as a code/API bug

evals.name: coop-coep-breaks-images
evals.prompt: After I added Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy: require-corp and Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin to get the Scandit engine running, the product images we load from our CDN stopped showing. How do I keep scanning working without breaking the cross-origin images?

assertions.text: Explains that COEP: require-corp blocks cross-origin subresources (the CDN images) that don't opt in via CORP/CORS
Frames cross-origin isolation / the multithreaded engine as the higher-performance path worth keeping, and encourages configuring COOP/COEP correctly rather than dropping them by default
Recommends keeping isolation and fixing the images while preserving it — CORP (Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy: cross-origin) / CORS + crossorigin attribute, or switching to COEP: credentialless
Mentions the single-threaded fallback (self-host same-origin + drop COOP/COEP) only as a last resort when isolation cannot be configured, noting reduced performance
Does not suggest removing or relocating the product images as the fix
